title: Ferrari strike staggering €100 million mark as Verstappen backs FIA
summary:
The news of Charles Leclerc's new contract has seen Ferrari's combined driver salary bill surpass €100 million, the first time this has happened in Formula 1! On media day ahead of this weekend's Monaco Grand Prix, the home hero explained just why he signed a new deal!

Elsewhere, Max Verstappen outlined his support of the FIA to make the right changes for 2027, while Lewis Hamilton downplayed Ferrari entering this weekend's race as the favourites for victory.

Join RacingNews365's Nick Golding and Fergal Walsh as they dissect Monaco GP media day!

title: Why Mercedes drivers resist team orders in growing F1 title fight
